I want to take a moment to express appreciation for her dedication and commitment to providing exceptional care to our oncology patients. Her expertise and compassion make a meaningful difference in their lives.
, I've observed several positive practices in her approach to patient care, particularly her meticulous attention to safety protocols and her emphasis on clear communication with patients and colleagues alike. Her dedication to ensuring a safe and supportive environment is commendable and aligns perfectly with our shared goal of delivering the highest standard of care.

As a breast imaging radiologist, I am dedicated to providing compassionate and thorough care to all my patients. I prioritize their well–being by ensuring accurate diagnoses and effective treatment plans. And as a chair of radiology department, I strongly believe in fostering a culture of safety and collaboration within my team, where constructive feedback is encouraged to enhance our practice and patient care standards. Creating a positive workplace environment is essential to me, as it directly impacts the quality of care we deliver. I am committed to remaining grounded and approachable, striving to build trust and confidence among both colleagues and patients alike.

Dr. Mukbil is a highly regarded neuroradiologist known for his professionalism, dedication, and extensive experience. He has made significant contributions to his department, including serving as interim chair, where he demonstrated strong leadership and a commitment to excellence. Dr. Mukbil's clinical expertise and compassionate approach to patient care have earned him admiration from colleagues and patients alike.

Dr. Nabil Khoury is recognized for his proficiency in MSK ultrasound and his unwavering dedication to delivering exceptional patient care. He is esteemed by both colleagues and patients for his compassionate demeanor.
Dr. Maurice Haddad is often covering double rotations in ultrasound and pediatric services. His commitment to his profession is evident in his tireless efforts to provide comprehensive care.

Dr. Mohamad Haidar is recognized for his expertise in nuclear medicine, particularly in treatment methods. He is highly regarded for his exemplary leadership and commitment to advancing medical practices. Dr. Haidar's dedication to both academic leadership and specialized nuclear medicine underscores his significant impact in these fields, making him a respected figure in healthcare and academia alike.

Dr. Roula Hourani, a respected figure in neuroradiology, is admired for her professional excellence, dedication. She is highly esteemed by colleagues and patients alike for her exceptional clinical skills and compassionate patient care. Dr. Hourani's commitment to advancing neuroradiology is underscored by her meticulous approach and continuous pursuit of knowledge in her field. Her recent recognition with an award for her outstanding clinical work further highlights her dedication and contribution to the medical community.

Dr. Lara Nassar is a highly respected breast imaging radiologist known for her compassionate patient care and professionalism. She prioritizes patient comfort and well–being, ensuring each person receives exceptional treatment. Dr. Nassar's dedication to advancing breast imaging is evident in her thorough approach and commitment to staying updated with the latest practices.

Dr. Hicham Moukaddam is highly admired for his exceptional patient care. He is an excellent body and musculoskeletal (MSK) radiologist and is appreciated by both colleagues and patients for his professional and compassionate approach.

Dr. Bassem Mourany is a distinguished specialist in cardiothoracic radiology known for his unwavering dedication and exceptional work ethic. As the head of the KMC radiology department, he has assumed the responsibility with great competence and has consistently delivered outstanding results. Dr. Mourany's leadership is characterized by his meticulous attention to detail and his commitment to maintaining high standards of patient care and operational efficiency.

Dr. Nadim Muallem is highly regarded as an interventional radiologist, known for his dedication and expertise in the field. He excels in interventional radiology procedures, demonstrating a commitment to providing high–quality patient care. Dr. Muallem's adept teaching abilities and rapport with colleagues highlight his role as a respected mentor and leader in the field of radiology.

Dr. Mustafa Natout is widely admired for his dedication to GI and ER radiology, where he excels in providing comprehensive patient care. His expertise in teaching is highly valued, as evidenced by his effective mentorship and the strong rapport he builds with residents. Dr. Natout's commitment to education and his exceptional clinical skills make him a trusted figure among colleagues and a favorite among residents.

Dr. Riad Khouzami is highly regarded for his exceptional versatility and dedication within the department. He adeptly covers rotations in neuroradiology, MSK, and IR, showcasing his broad expertise and reliability. As the associate chair for safety and quality, Riad has made significant contributions, ensuring high standards and efficient operations.

Dr. Christel Tamer, specializing in neuroradiology, is esteemed for her professionalism, dedication, and personable demeanor. Dr. Tamer's commitment to advancing neuroradiology is evident in her meticulous attention to detail and her proactive efforts to stay at the forefront of her field.

Dr. Aline Geara, an ER radiologist, is known for her hard work and dedication. She handles the demanding ER workload without complaint and covers shifts twice a week, demonstrating her commitment to patient care and team support. Her reliability and dedication make her an essential part of the emergency radiology team.

Known for her compassionate approach, she prioritizes the well–being and comfort of her patients, ensuring they receive the highest standard of care. Dr. Hobeika's ability to foster a positive environment and her dedication to excellence make her a valued member of the medical team.

Dr. Reine Nader, a highly regarded breast imaging radiologist, is respected by both colleagues and staff for her professional demeanor and dedication to patient care. Her approach is marked by a commitment to providing exceptional service to her patients, and she is particularly noted for her expertise in fetal MRI.

Dr. Ngheioui is also highly regarded for his role in teaching and mentoring residents, contributing significantly to the education and training of future radiology professionals.

Professional Behavior:

	1.	Punctuality: Always arrives on time for appointments and meetings.
	2.	Communication: Clearly explains diagnoses and treatment plans to patients and their families, ensuring they understand.
	3.	Empathy: Shows compassion and understanding towards patients’ concerns and conditions.
	4.	Teamwork: Collaborates effectively with colleagues, nurses, and other healthcare professionals.
	5.	Continuing Education: Regularly participates in professional development and stays updated with the latest medical advancements.
	6.	Patient Advocacy: Prioritizes patient needs and advocates for their best interests.
	7.	Ethical Practice: Adheres to ethical guidelines and maintains patient confidentiality.

I am writing to express my concerns regarding Dr. Barazi's role as Program Director. Throughout my residency, I have experienced significant challenges under her leadership that have profoundly affected my well–being and professional development.

Dr. Barazi has displayed favoritism towards certain residents, overlooking valid concerns raised by myself and others regarding bullying and exclusion. Despite being aware of these issues, she evaluated me based on biased reports without addressing the underlying problems. This approach has left me feeling insecure and unfairly treated.

Her management style has included threats of probation, frequent exams, and unnecessary medical interventions, such as prescribing antidepressants without proper evaluation. This has exacerbated my anxiety and isolated me from the resident community.

Moreover, Dr. Barazi's unprofessional conduct includes inappropriate remarks about my performance, discussions with other residents about my evaluations, and unjustified grading discrepancies despite positive feedback from other attendings.

I believe Dr. Barazi's actions are detrimental to the program and residents' well–being. I urge you to investigate these concerns and take appropriate action to ensure a fair and supportive environment for all residents.

In my humble opinion, having been part of this department for six years, I truly and honestly believe that Dr. Korban is one of the most, if not the most, valuable and exceptionally skilled members and pillars of this department. Dr. Korban exemplifies time management and efficiency. She is sought after from every borough, county, and district in Lebanon, as well as surrounding countries. She is consistently referred challenging and critical cases from many successful and renowned surgeons. This is not only because Dr. Korban embodies ethics and compassion, treating every patient with the utmost respect, honesty, and professionalism, but also because she is a truly gifted mind and surgeon. Her skills always astonish us, as she possesses extremely advanced surgical skills and surgical IQ, which gives her the confidence to safely approach the rarest and most challenging cases that have been unsuccessfully treated in many centers.
Dr. Korban always responds to our calls, regardless of the case, day, or time, even when she is not on call. She is the first to rush in if a certain attending is not reachable, making us residents feel supported and protected. No matter the urgency, she is next to us, ensuring the utmost safety and advanced treatment for any patient. Dr. Korban has matched her exceptional training in skull base surgery and rhinology with becoming an allergy expert, forming an extremely rare and well–rounded physician capable of tackling the most advanced cases from A to Z.
Dr. Korban is an inspiration and a true trailblazer. She frequently participates in worldwide renowned conferences, invited countless times as a guest speaker, always delivering new and cutting–edge advancements in her field. She has always treated all the staff with the utmost respect, always distributing gifts of gratitude from patients to the staff, especially the nursing staff. Every nurse fights for time with Dr. Korban, at least from what I have seen, because she is so attentive, caring, and respectful. Dr. Korban's clinic is a critical part of this program, offering an in–depth look at patient care in the best possible way. She consistently follows the latest evidence–based guidelines, always offering maximal medical therapy before opting for surgery. Despite her fearlessness and skill, she is ethical, ensuring the patient receives optimal treatment and leaving no doubt about what more she could have done.
Dr. Korban stays late during clinical days because she always accepts drop–ins, urgent cases, and late patients, often sacrificing extra hours from her personal life. She is always critical in ensuring we improve and are efficient in the OR, teaching us amazing techniques and challenging us every step of the way.

Unfortunately, Dr. Mourad never gave me a fair chance. He came to the program with preconceived notions about me, constantly stating that I have poor relationships with staff, residents, patients, and attendings. The truth is, I have received many texts and messages praising my support and guidance from various team members throughout the year. I have a good relationship with most of the attendings, except for Dr. Mourad and one other. I have been praised by staff for going out of my way to help them, even with personal problems, and for my patient care during OPD and OR.
Dr. Mourad has taught me a lot, even though he has always shown signs of not liking me personally through passive–aggressive comments. He constantly waits for any small misunderstanding or technical problem to report to the program director. He never personally talks to me or discusses issues at the time but instead writes a report on everything he sees wrong with me. Dr. Mourad showed great disparity between the chiefs, even though the chiefs themselves were on good terms. This was evident in many events reported to the program director.
For example, he would talk aggressively with Mounir and not respect his surroundings, while never doing so with Nadine, even when discussing similar situations. It was clear that this was due to Dr. Mourad's constant need for Dr. Nadine's father to assist him in complicated cases, while he felt there would be no repercussions for talking down to Dr. Bousaab. This was especially evident in two cases. In one, Dr. Bousaab complained about how he was addressed when asking a scheduling question and was berated in front of patients and staff. The program director's response was, "Dr. Mourad was mad about something, and it came out on you." In a second event, Dr. Bousaab was berated in the OR for imaging that was not uploaded to the appropriate system, which was not his fault. When Dr. Bousaab informed the program director, he was told it would be taken care of, only to receive no response. Instead, a few days later, he was called to take a warning due in part to an email from Dr. Mourad concerning events dating back months, seemingly in retaliation for the complaint.
I have never seen such actions in a reputable hospital and am saddened that Dr. Mourad went down this path. He seemed to be ethical and have a good moral compass. He always recognized my surgical skills and mentioned them positively. It's a shame he didn't have the self–confidence to judge for himself. It's truly a shame he was dragged down such a path that has become so evident to all the residents, even if they are too fearful to mention it themselves. I have been fearful for years to stand up to these actions, fearing losing my job or any further opportunities. I hope Dr. Mourad changes things around. He has great potential but seems to lack a strong independent moral personality and tends to follow the path of least resistance when judging residents. His behavior shows a tendency to pick on and bully those he perceives as weak, which is detrimental to the mental health of many residents.

Having had the fortune and privilege to train under and learn from Dr. Hadi, I now have a deepened respect for the teaching aspect of a residency program. Dr. Hadi is an unparalleled mentor who is irreplaceable. I feel an unwavering sense of security in cases with Dr. Hadi due to his exceptional breadth of knowledge and experience across all aspects of our specialty, despite his specialization as a magnificent rhinologist and skull base surgeon. Dr. Hadi is also one of the most compassionate and ethical physicians I have had the honor of working with. Not once has Dr. Hadi made me feel any different from my co–chief, Dr. Nadin El Hadi, his own daughter. On the contrary, he consistently treats us equally and ensures we learn to work together and with others more effectively. He has even made sure that I scrubbed in on major cases assigned to Dr. Nadin, as is her right as chief of the week.
Dr. Hadi has always been present and ready for any urgent cases and consultations, even when he is not on call or in the area. I have seen Dr. Hadi come to the rescue in many urgent cases being handled by other attendings in our department, always willing to help and lend his knowledge. As residents, we all know we can count on Dr. Hadi to tackle the most challenging cases that have been given up on and to treat them with dignity, always giving his all.
The rhinology program developed by Dr. Hadi alongside Dr. Korban is one of the best and most advanced I have seen. Dr. Hadi and Dr. Korban use all their resources and experts in different fields to conduct an amazing collaborative learning experience that provides us with various international schools of thought. As residents, my colleagues and I have often discussed how amazing this program is and how privileged we are to be a part of it. This program connects us with world–renowned surgeons globally. Dr. Korban and Dr. Hadi both stay hours after lectures, volunteering their precious time for us to ask questions and discuss topics. This support and commitment to teaching is unmatched.
On a humanitarian, ethical, and compassionate level, Dr. Hadi has always shown me encouragement and support. He has always been fair, providing constructive feedback and never letting me get away with any mistakes in my notes, knowledge, or how I handle urgent consults. Even at 3 a.m. on a consult, he ensures the patient is safe and afterwards goes over every step, discussing what was done right or wrong and how to improve in the most constructive and motivating way.
Dr. Hadi is truly irreplaceable and would be a great loss if he ever left this program, especially in these times when he holds a significant burden in the department. Dr. Hadi is a rare and remarkable type of surgeon who is well–rounded and dedicated to teaching, always taking more of his time to allow us more exposure and hands–on experience. He is truly one of the greatest mentors, physicians, surgeons, and men I have had the pleasure of meeting.

The current institutional environment incentivizes physicians to see an increasing number of patients in order to boost their income. While this approach may enhance financial performance in the short term, it often comes at the expense of patient care quality and overall satisfaction. This high–pressure setting can lead to rushed consultations, reduced time for thorough patient evaluations, and a lack of personalized care.
To protect an enhance the institution’s reputation and ensure a higher standard of patient care, a shift in priorities is essential. Emphasizing patient satisfaction and comprehensive care should be the primary focus. By doing so, the institution can foster a more positive and trusting relationship between patients and healthcare providers.
Additionally, many physicians are currently neglecting to write an appropriate note and update patients' problem lists and medication records in the Epic system. This oversight can result in significant medical errors, including the prescription of duplicate medications and the occurrence of adverse drug interactions. Maintaining accurate and up–to–date records is crucial for ensuring patient safety and delivering effective care.
